text.title: LIVING IN BEIJING, CHINA (as a Foreigner) 🇨🇳 First Week Studying Chinese!
text.u: good morning today is our fourth day
here in Beijing leaving and studying we
on our way to school right now and join
us over the next couple of days we'll
show you what our typical ordinary day
is like as students here in Beijing
China
[Music]
it takes us about 45 minutes to get to
school and it seems like the rash hour
starts about
7:30 figured that one out need to make
sure that we always leave before
that it's the middle of October in
Beijing at the moment which means the
weather is starting to cool down but
it's still in that phase where some days
it's quite warm and sunny and yesterday
it was 20° and sunny today it's a lot
cooler it's 10° I think at the moment
and quite Misty and cloudy it's very
autumnal feeling and I think within a
couple of weeks it's going to be a lot
colder and a lot more
winterlik and on that note the time is
528 school is on the sixth floor and
starts at 8:00 so let's go upstairs
oh H wow that was
quick look what language English English
English English
[Music]
you remember what
me not so good
is together subject you should
use plur do you understand
because
[Laughter]
always even
today just wanted to mention something
quickly we're not here to promote the
school by the way or do anything like
that we really wanted to have this
experience and learn Mandarin we
actually found LTL ourselves and since
we're having such a wonderful time here
we spoke to the team here and let them
know that we obviously filming videos
and asked if they could give some cool
things to our subscribers and here what
we've got if you are a new student you
can get 50% off flexi online classes
okay flexi online classes and if you
want to have the same experience as us
and come actually have a hom stay or
whatever just stay here and attend the
school you can have 10 free flexi
classes after you finish your course
yeah the link we'll leave in the
description and we'll pin the comment
with it
in Beijing um I have a
question uh beer is
p does the first the first is the same
SK different
character did you want to eat together
[Music]
byee want I
put
nice do you know why you ordered it
from we got it in person from there's a
mall across the Street
yeah it's the mall with mini so in the
on the first floor
so
yeah let's try
Okay byebye bye bye byebye bye
J we just had a quick lunch and headed
back home it started to rain so we had
to run around and over the last couple
of days we've been just working in a
cafe near to our school and then heading
home in the evening but tonight we're
headed home early
and going to work at home because
tonight we're actually going out when we
were talking about like planning our
days you know just getting excited by
going to school we thought oh my God
we're just going to have the whole day
free we're going to be starting only
from 8 till 12 like 4 hours and then we
have so much time so much
energy doing these 4 hours maybe because
it's the first week as well and we're
figuring getting used to everything and
figuring everything out but it's
actually so much we feel quite exhausted
after these 4 hours especially I think I
hit the wall yesterday so today I feel a
bit better but these 4 hours it's
amazing there's so much going on so much
information and I feel
like I know quite a few words already
however I can spell them in pinging
pinging is the Latin spelling of Chinese
characters but I really struggle to
pronounce them
so maybe I'll be talking to people by
just writing and pinging maybe no one
will understand me probably but yeah I
guess we're getting there also it's
quite hard for me to receive that much
information and not to expect from
myself to know it straight away just to
take it easy and chill we really
enjoying it but it's a lot it's the hat
is spinning pretty much all the time so
yeah we're just going to head back home
um work do a little bit of a ex
sizes and uh head
out you
have H now walking up to the sixth
floor free
house party
this is how many modes we have after 4
days I want to separate it all so useful
words and phrases that we need to use
all the time and to use to build
sentences write them down separately
because everything is kind of mixed up
and when I need to reply or ask
something just takes forever to find
them just want to organize it properly
and hopefully that will make everything
easier make sense more comp compiz it
how to say compartmentalize
it
compartmentalize comp
compartmentalize comp compartmentalize
compartment compartmental
compart compartmentalize it
compartmental compart compartmentalize
it compartmentalize
it I think I'm going to edit now do
something that I know how to do sat down
to work for a bit somehow 4 hours went
by didn't do any exercises and it's time
to leave so let's head out we actually
wanted to join the gym near to our
school uh we checked one across the road
and it was 100 per person so I think I'm
going to stick to doing exercises at
home yeah let's go and have
dinner I think it's picking duck on the
menu
today where does the time go it's
actually scary I thought we sat there I
don't know for half an hour
yeah honestly I was minding my own
business and then all of a sudden bang I
was like oh my God it's 5:15 we need to
go
crazy just ordered a DD taxi to take us
to where we're going to have dinner
tonight and apparently
it's 300 M
away oh God
bye-bye that was
long now
this is swear like this
so here we have some
to duck I'll take a video like some
Greens Walnut chicken which I just tried
it's
amazing on
sun and a lovely company
huh okay I
[Music]
FR so when he receive praise
I'm trying
to censor
myself okay
[Music]
[Music]
uh
no
five we say
first number how to
theing yeah so how many years ago you go
d square last year oh junor
Jun uh we
say lost
see you next
Monday the first week is complete our
teaching is so sweet yesterday we were
practicing in um buying and like selling
fruit and at the end we asked to the
price like of how much the fruits are
going to cost and you're like too
expensive and she stood there justifi
like this is an import don't buy keepy
me they're too expensive and it's so
funny she was explaining everything
breaking stuff down she's amazing she's
helping us so much and explains
everything so well so we're just going
to grab some food quickly
[Music]
and after we're actually going to
head
to tenan square we're going to head
inside the Forbidden City for the first
time so
hungry want to go upstairs yeah
we made it to the end of the week quite
honestly I don't remember the last time
we actually paid attention to the days
of the week when you run your own
business and when you're kind of
traveling all the time time every day is
the same but this is the first time that
I feel like I need a weekend in a long
long time it's been an amazing week I
feel like we've learned so much but yeah
so ready for that
weekend we just arrived here at tenan
Square it took us an hour to get fre
Security checks last time it was
literally no time we went through a
straightway and apparently this is not a
usual theme but the queue wasn't even
that big however it took us an hour and
we're finally
here you know it was near enough a year
ago we were here on tenanan Square near
enough exactly a year ago that day
looked a little bit different
weatherwise it was very very sunny that
day day and now it's a little bit
overcast I just remembered what we were
talking about we were talking about
coming back to China in tring different
can you imagine and we're literally a
year later we're coming back to tenan
square having been to I think seven
different provinces at this point
there's still a hell of a lot more to
explore and we need to get the language
mastered but we're on our way you know
our it's incredible to think that just a
year ago we were standing here and this
place is just as impressive as it was
one year ago we've got the soldiers
doing the march at the
moment the big imposing picture of
Sherman Mau in
front what a place what a place you feel
like you're in the very center of China
in the very center of an Empire here and
actually we're not here alone today
there is six of us four other students
four students including us and then two
of the guys from LT
and we're going to go inside the
Forbidden City which is something we
didn't do last year so excited so so
excited we read so much about this place
in particular we were actually watching
a documentary recently when we had food
poisoning in Xian on the fitt city so
yeah
incredible not the food poisoning the
poisoning
interesting definitely the documentary
[Music]
didn't go
[Music]
[Music]
there welcome inside the Forbidden
City we are currently walking along this
bit of pavement which may look
insignificant but this is the center
line through the middle of Beijing
apparently and from here D Singh airport
is 40 km away to the South and in a
completely straight line so in the
airport the new airport they've built
you can see this line and it says 40 km
from here is the very center is the
Forbidden City the very center of
Beijing fascinating
we weren't actually inside the Forbidden
City a minute ago but we're coming
inside to it now we've just gone through
a final ticket check and welcome to the
Forbidden
City wow it's
big it is huge what she
said what did you say that's what she
said
[Music]
whye spee
[Music]
[Music]
in his time this place was completely
shut off to Outsiders so the name the
Forbidden City means it came about
obviously because it was shut off to the
members of the public to commoners an
entire section of the center of Beijing
was completely off bounds fascinating
and
now obviously it's not only normal for
Chinese tourists but there's people from
all over the world here walking through
its buildings and around its grounds and
this place is huge apparently there are
99,999 rooms
here
what and I've been to the O2
Arena was the real
[Music]
winner fore
[Music]
see M see you Monday see M have a good
weekend see you later have a good
weekend just made it back home and
somehow it's 9:00 it Jed back took a
while I wanted to say that it can be
quite social being here in school you
can keep yourself to yourself if you
want to but you can also join different
activities that students organize
themselves and people that work in
school and what we really like as well
it seems like there are people of all
different ages also there are lots of
people from all over the world well like
we met Germans lots of Italians Russians
British American so people from all over
the place and I wanted to mention that
Chinese people really love
durian and you know what we've seen
everywhere durian Pizza being sold
literally all over the place so we're
thinking to order durian pizza for
everyone to try at school I think it'll
be quite fun to see everyone's reactions
sounds Grim but I really want to try it
but anyway we're going to leave you here
for now I think we're ready for bed
thanks for joining us and see you in the
next one
